<!-- Generated by `make cli-reference` (scripts/gen-cli-reference.sh) from each
     tool's --help. Do not edit by hand; re-run after changing a tool's flags. -->

# CLI reference

Every user-facing command in this toolkit. Each block is the tool's own `--help`
output, so this file stays in sync with the code (`make check` fails if it drifts).

## `tools/ados-ollama`

```text
Usage: ados-ollama <command> [options] [-- <sub-tool args>]

Commands:
  doctor       Read-only preflight check
  install      Install ADOS into the target project
  setup        Ensure Ollama is running and a Gemma model is pulled
  configure    Write the hybrid .opencode/opencode.jsonc into the target
  sandbox      Launch OpenCode in an isolated Docker sandbox
  all          doctor (advisory) -> install -> setup -> configure

Options:
  --target <dir>      Target project (default: .)
  --model <tag>       Local Gemma model (forwarded to setup + configure)
  --cloud-model <id>  Cloud provider/model for high-stakes agents
  --num-ctx <n>       Local context window
  --cloud-only        All agents on the cloud model (configure/all)
  --local-only        All agents on the local Ollama model (configure/all)
  -n, --dry-run       Show what would be done without doing it
  -v, --verbose       Debug output
  -h, --help          Show this help
  -V, --version       Show version
  --                  Forward the remaining args to the sub-tool

Examples:
  ados-ollama all --target . --model gemma3:27b
  ados-ollama sandbox --target . -- --env ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-...
```

## `tools/ados-ollama-doctor`

```text
Usage: ados-ollama-doctor [options]

Read-only preflight: checks the host tools needed to run ADOS-on-Ollama and
prints how to fix anything missing. Changes nothing.

Options:
  -h, --help          Show this help
  -V, --version       Show version
      --model <tag>   Gemma model tag to check for (default: gemma3)
```

## `scripts/setup-ollama.sh`

```text
Usage: setup-ollama [options]

Ensure Ollama is running and a Gemma model is available, then verify it
responds. Idempotent: an already-present model is not re-pulled.

Options:
  --model <tag>     Gemma model to ensure (default: gemma3)
  --num-ctx <n>     Context window for verification (default: 32000)
  --verify-tools    Also probe tool-calling (best-effort; WARNs if unsupported)
  --no-start        Do not try to start a local Ollama server
  -f, --force       Re-pull the model even if present
  -n, --dry-run     Show what would be done without doing it
  -v, --verbose     Debug output
  -h, --help        Show this help
  -V, --version     Show version
```

## `scripts/install-ados.sh`

```text
Usage: install-ados --target <dir> [options]

Install ADOS into a target project: ADOS's docs/templates/.ai (via its own
--local installer) PLUS the agent/command definitions copied project-local so a
Docker sandbox can see them, with an ADOS provenance record. Idempotent.

Options:
  --target <dir>     Project to install into (default: .)
  --ados-repo <dir>  ADOS checkout to copy from (default: auto-detected)
  --global           Also run ADOS's global install (~/.config/opencode)
  -f, --force        Pass --force to ADOS (overwrite project-specific files)
  -n, --dry-run      Show what would be done without doing it
  -v, --verbose      Debug output
  -h, --help         Show this help
  -V, --version      Show version
```

## `tools/gen-opencode-config`

```text
Usage: gen-opencode-config --target <dir> [options]

Write <target>/.opencode/opencode.jsonc with the hybrid model map (local Gemma
via Ollama + cloud for high-stakes agents). Deterministic and idempotent; an
existing differing file is preserved unless --force.

Options:
  --target <dir>       Project to write into (default: .)
  --local-model <tag>  Ollama model for local agents (default: gemma3)
  --cloud-model <id>   provider/model for cloud agents (default: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6)
  --ollama-url <url>   baseURL for Ollama (default: http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1)
  --num-ctx <n>        Context window for the local provider (default: 32000)
  --cloud-only         Put ALL agents on the cloud model (no local agents)
  --local-only         Put ALL agents on the local Ollama model
  -f, --force          Overwrite an existing, differing config
  -n, --dry-run        Show what would be done without doing it
  -v, --verbose        Debug output
  -h, --help           Show this help
  -V, --version        Show version
```

## `tools/ados-sandbox`

```text
Usage: ados-sandbox --target <dir> [options]

Run OpenCode for a target project inside an isolated Docker (Linux microVM)
sandbox, wired to host Ollama (host.docker.internal) and the project's hybrid
.opencode/opencode.jsonc.

Prerequisites:
  * Host Ollama must listen on 0.0.0.0 (OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0:11434), not 127.0.0.1.
  * Size the VM in Docker Desktop > Resources (no per-sandbox CPU/mem flag).

Options:
  --target <dir>      Project to run (default: .)
  --name <name>       Sandbox name (default: ados-<dirname>)
  --ollama-url <url>  OLLAMA_BASE_URL inside the sandbox
                      (default: http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1)
  --allow-host <h>    Extra host to allow through the egress proxy (repeatable)
  --env KEY=VAL       Env var to inject (repeatable; e.g. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...)
  --env-file <f>      File of KEY=VAL env vars to inject
  --use-run           Launch via 'docker sandbox run' (no env injection)
  --no-launch         Create + configure but do not launch OpenCode
  -n, --dry-run       Show what would be done without doing it
  -v, --verbose       Debug output
  -h, --help          Show this help
  -V, --version       Show version
```

## `tools/ados-sandbox-macos`

```text
Usage: ados-sandbox-macos --target <dir> [--allow-write <path>]... [-- <cmd ...>]

Run a command (default: opencode) under a macOS Seatbelt profile that confines
file WRITES to the target project (+ temp and opencode/ollama/ados caches).
EXPERIMENTAL — sandbox-exec is deprecated and weaker than the Docker sandbox;
use ados-sandbox (Docker) for real isolation, this only for macOS-native work.

Options:
  --target <dir>        Project whose tree may be written (default: .)
  --allow-write <path>  Extra writable subpath (repeatable)
  -n, --dry-run         Print the profile and command without running
  -v, --verbose         Debug output
  -h, --help            Show this help
  -V, --version         Show version
```

## `scripts/setup-dev.sh`

```text
Usage: setup-dev [options]

Install the Homebrew packages needed to develop the ADOS-on-Ollama toolkit:
  bash (>=5), shellcheck, shfmt, bats-core

Options:
  -h, --help     Show this help
  -n, --dry-run  Show what would be installed without doing it
```
